distinguish_from.Saponite: Nontronite 2.29um Fe-OH vs Saponite 2.31-2.32um Mg-OH; nontronite shows stronger 1.43um feature
distinguish_from.Fe smectite: Nontronite has sharper, deeper 2.29um band; Fe smectite is broader and shallower

Nontronite.mars_context: Abundant in Nili Fossae, Mawrth Vallis; indicates aqueous alteration of basaltic materials
Nontronite.spectral_description: Strong absorptions at 1.4 and 1.9um from structural water, with diagnostic Fe3+-OH band at 2.29um. Continuum-removed spectrum shows deep, narrow 2.29um feature distinguishing it from Mg-bearing smectites.

band_assignments.2.28: Fe-OH stretch + bend combination (broader than nontronite)

distinguish_from.Nontronite: Fe smectite has broader, shallower 2.28um band vs sharp 2.29um in nontronite
distinguish_from.Fe/Mg smectite: Fe smectite lacks distinct Mg-OH shoulder near 2.32um

Fe smectite.mars_context: Widespread in Noachian terrains; forms from low-temperature aqueous alteration
Fe smectite.spectral_description: Similar to nontronite but with broader Fe-OH absorption near 2.28um. Water features at 1.4 and 1.9um present. Less crystalline than nontronite.

band_assignments.1.39: OH stretch overtone (Mg-OH shifts to shorter wavelength)

distinguish_from.Nontronite: Saponite 2.31um Mg-OH vs Nontronite 2.29um Fe-OH; ~20nm shift is diagnostic
distinguish_from.Serpentine: Saponite has 1.9um H2O band; serpentine lacks or has weak 1.9um
distinguish_from.Vermiculite: Very similar spectrally; saponite has slightly sharper 2.31um

Saponite.mars_context: Found in association with olivine-bearing terrains; indicates aqueous alteration of Mg-rich rocks
Saponite.spectral_description: Mg-OH absorption at 2.31um distinguishes from Fe-bearing smectites. 1.4 and 1.9um water features present. The ~20nm shift from 2.29 to 2.31um is the key Fe vs Mg smectite discriminator.

distinguish_from.Kaolinite: Montmorillonite single 2.21um band vs kaolinite doublet at 2.16+2.21um
distinguish_from.Al smectite: Very similar; montmorillonite may show stronger 1.9um

Montmorillonite.mars_context: Detected in Mawrth Vallis layered deposits; suggests prolonged water interaction
Montmorillonite.spectral_description: Characterized by Al-OH absorption at 2.21um. Distinguishable from kaolinite by single absorption vs doublet. Strong water features at 1.4 and 1.9um indicate hydrated structure.

distinguish_from.Saponite: Very similar spectrally; vermiculite may have broader 2.3um feature and stronger interlayer water bands

Vermiculite.mars_context: Rare detection; possible weathering product of mafic minerals
Vermiculite.spectral_description: Mg-bearing phyllosilicate with 2.32um absorption. Spectrally very similar to saponite but may show broader features due to variable interlayer cation composition.

band_assignments.2.30: Mixed Fe-OH and Mg-OH (intermediate between 2.29 and 2.31)

distinguish_from.Nontronite: Fe/Mg smectite 2.30um is intermediate vs pure Fe 2.29um
distinguish_from.Saponite: Fe/Mg smectite 2.30um is intermediate vs pure Mg 2.31um

Fe/Mg smectite.mars_context: Most common phyllosilicate detection on Mars; widespread in Noachian crust
Fe/Mg smectite.spectral_description: Shows Metal-OH absorption at intermediate position (~2.30um) between Fe-end (2.29) and Mg-end (2.31). Indicates mixed Fe/Mg composition in octahedral sites.

distinguish_from.Montmorillonite: Nearly identical; generic Al smectite designation

Al smectite.mars_context: Upper stratigraphic layers in Mawrth Vallis; suggests advanced weathering
Al smectite.spectral_description: Al-OH bearing smectite with diagnostic 2.20um absorption. Distinguished from Fe/Mg smectites by absorption position (~100nm blueward shift).

distinguish_from.Montmorillonite: Kaolinite has diagnostic doublet at 2.16+2.21um vs single band at 2.21um

Kaolinite.mars_context: Rare on Mars; found in Mawrth Vallis and some crater deposits; implies acidic leaching
Kaolinite.spectral_description: Unique doublet absorption at 2.16 and 2.21um from two distinct Al-OH environments (inner hydroxyl and inner surface hydroxyl). This doublet is the key diagnostic feature.

distinguish_from.Saponite: Chlorite has additional 2.25um feature and lacks strong 1.9um H2O band
distinguish_from.Serpentine: Similar 2.32-2.33um but chlorite has broader features and 2.25um shoulder

Chlorite.mars_context: Found in Noachian terrains; associated with hydrothermal alteration
Chlorite.spectral_description: Shows multiple Metal-OH absorptions. Distinguished from smectites by weaker or absent 1.9um H2O band and presence of 2.25um shoulder from brucite-like interlayer.

band_assignments.1.48: OH stretch overtone (shifted from typical phyllosilicate 1.4um)

distinguish_from.Montmorillonite: Prehnite 2.24um vs montmorillonite 2.21um; prehnite OH overtone at 1.48 not 1.41um
distinguish_from.Al smectite: Shifted OH overtone and different 2.2um position

Prehnite.mars_context: Nili Fossae region; indicates higher temperature alteration (>200C) than smectites
Prehnite.spectral_description: Hydrated calcium aluminosilicate with shifted OH features. The 1.48um overtone (vs typical 1.41um) and 2.24um Al-OH position are diagnostic. Indicates hydrothermal alteration conditions.

distinguish_from.Polyhydr. sulfate: Kieserite 2.07-2.13um vs polyhydrated 1.93-1.97um (monohydrated shifts H2O band redward)
distinguish_from.Gypsum: Kieserite single shifted H2O vs gypsum sharp 1.94um + additional bands

Kieserite.mars_context: Valles Marineris ILDs (Interior Layered Deposits); formed by evaporation of sulfate-rich brines
Kieserite.spectral_description: Monohydrated Mg-sulfate distinguished by redshifted water absorption at 2.07-2.13um instead of typical 1.9um. This shift is diagnostic of single structural water molecule.

distinguish_from.Bassanite: Gypsum has 1.75um band and triplet at 2.2um; bassanite is broader/shifted
distinguish_from.Polyhydr. sulfate: Gypsum has distinctive triplet and 1.75um not seen in generic polyhydrated sulfates

Gypsum.mars_context: North polar dunes, some equatorial deposits; indicates evaporite deposition
Gypsum.spectral_description: Rich spectral signature with multiple diagnostic features. The 1.75um band and 2.2um triplet are unique to gypsum among sulfates. Five distinct absorption bands make it one of the most spectrally distinctive minerals.

band_assignments.1.94: H2O combination (similar to gypsum but less structured)

distinguish_from.Gypsum: Bassanite lacks 1.75um band and 2.2um triplet seen in gypsum; fewer water features due to hemihydrate nature

Bassanite.mars_context: Proposed in some Valles Marineris deposits; intermediate dehydration product
Bassanite.spectral_description: Hemihydrate calcium sulfate with fewer water absorption features than gypsum. Lacks the diagnostic 1.75um and 2.2um triplet of gypsum.

distinguish_from.Calcite: Magnesite 2.30+2.50um vs calcite 2.34+2.53um (Mg vs Ca shifts bands to shorter wavelengths)
distinguish_from.Fe/Mg smectite: Carbonate lacks 1.9um H2O band; has 3.4um CO3 feature

Magnesite.mars_context: Nili Fossae; suggests carbonate formation from CO2-rich fluids or atmosphere
Magnesite.spectral_description: Diagnostic CO3 absorptions at 2.30 and 2.50um, with additional features at 3.4-3.5um. Shorter wavelength position than calcite due to lighter Mg cation. Lacks water features.

distinguish_from.Magnesite: Calcite bands shifted ~40nm redward (2.34 vs 2.30, 2.53 vs 2.50) due to heavier Ca cation

Calcite.mars_context: Rare surface detection; found mixed with other phases in some craters
Calcite.spectral_description: CO3 absorptions at redder positions than magnesite. The Ca cation mass shifts vibrational frequencies to longer wavelengths. Key bands at 2.34 and 2.53um.

distinguish_from.Goethite: Hematite 0.86um band vs goethite 0.90-0.93um; hematite lacks 3um OH band

Hematite.mars_context: Meridiani Planum (Opportunity landing site); forms by dehydration of goethite or direct precipitation
Hematite.spectral_description: Iron oxide characterized by crystal field absorptions in VNIR. In the IR range (1.0-3.9um used in CRISM), features are subtle broad absorptions. Primarily identified by steep red slope and weak broad features.

band_assignments.0.90: Fe3+ crystal field transition (shifted from hematite 0.86)

distinguish_from.Hematite: Goethite 0.90um vs hematite 0.86um; goethite has 3.0um OH band

Goethite.mars_context: Less common than hematite; indicates wetter formation conditions
Goethite.spectral_description: Iron oxyhydroxide with shifted crystal field band at 0.90um and diagnostic OH fundamental at 3.0um. Presence of OH distinguishes from anhydrous hematite.

band_assignments.1.05: Fe2+ crystal field (M2 site) - Band I
band_assignments.1.83: Fe2+ crystal field (M1 site) - Band II (shorter than HCP)

distinguish_from.HCP: LCP Band II at 1.83um vs HCP Band II at 2.15um; the ~300nm shift is diagnostic of Ca content
distinguish_from.Olivine: Pyroxene has two distinct bands; olivine has broad single complex near 1.05um

LCP.mars_context: Noachian highlands crust; primitive igneous rock composition
LCP.spectral_description: Low-calcium pyroxene (orthopyroxene/pigeonite) with two Fe2+ crystal field bands. Band II at 1.83um is shorter wavelength than HCP, reflecting lower Ca content in M2 crystallographic site.

band_assignments.1.05: Fe2+ crystal field (M2 site) - Band I
band_assignments.2.15: Fe2+ crystal field (M1 site) - Band II (longer than LCP)

distinguish_from.LCP: HCP Band II at 2.15um vs LCP Band II at 1.83um
distinguish_from.Fe/Mg smectite: HCP 2.15um is broader and centered differently than smectite 2.30um Metal-OH

HCP.mars_context: Volcanic flows, Hesperian-aged plains; indicates more evolved magmatism
HCP.spectral_description: High-calcium pyroxene (augite/diopside) with Band II shifted to 2.15um due to Ca in M2 site. The 300nm shift from LCP (1.83) to HCP (2.15) is the primary pyroxene classification criterion.

band_assignments.1.05: Fe2+ crystal field - broad complex absorption spanning 0.85-1.5um

distinguish_from.Pyroxene: Olivine has single broad complex near 1um; pyroxene has two distinct bands at 1.0 and 1.8-2.2um
distinguish_from.Olivine (Fo-rich): Fo-rich shifts band center to slightly shorter wavelength

Olivine.mars_context: Nili Fossae, Syrtis Major; indicates primitive mantle-derived rocks
Olivine.spectral_description: Broad, complex absorption centered near 1.05um spanning 0.85-1.5um from overlapping Fe2+ crystal field transitions in M1 and M2 sites. No discrete second band distinguishes from pyroxene.

distinguish_from.Magnesite: Generic Mg-carbonate class; may include magnesite and Mg-rich dolomite
distinguish_from.Fe/Mg smectite: Carbonate 2.30+2.50 pair vs smectite 2.30 only; carbonate has 3.4um CO3

Mg carbonate.mars_context: Nili Fossae; significant for understanding early Mars CO2 cycle
Mg carbonate.spectral_description: CO3-bearing phase with paired absorptions at 2.30 and 2.50um. Distinguished from phyllosilicates by absence of 1.9um H2O and presence of 3.4um CO3 band.

distinguish_from.Phyllosilicate: CO2 ice has sharp, narrow bands vs broader mineral OH absorptions; seasonal/polar distribution

CO2 ice.mars_context: Polar caps and seasonal deposits; major volatile reservoir
CO2 ice.spectral_description: Sharp, narrow absorption features diagnostic of solid CO2. Seasonal appearance/disappearance in polar regions helps identify. Distinguished from minerals by narrow band widths and spatial/temporal context.

distinguish_from.Fe/Mg smectite: Generic clay designation when specific smectite type cannot be determined

Fe/Mg clay.mars_context: Widespread Noachian detection; general Fe/Mg phyllosilicate when subtype is uncertain
Fe/Mg clay.spectral_description: Generic Fe/Mg-bearing phyllosilicate with typical 1.4, 1.9, and 2.3um absorptions but insufficient spectral detail to assign specific mineral name.

band_assignments.1.9: Fe2+ crystal field Band II (intermediate position)

distinguish_from.LCP: Generic pyroxene when LCP/HCP distinction unclear
distinguish_from.HCP: Band II position intermediate or ambiguous

Pyroxene.spectral_description: Generic pyroxene classification when Band II position does not clearly distinguish LCP vs HCP.

band_assignments.2.20: Fe2+ crystal field Band II (further shifted by higher Ca)

distinguish_from.HCP: High-Ca variant has Band II shifted further to ~2.20um (vs standard HCP 2.15um)

HCP (high-Ca).spectral_description: High-Ca pyroxene endmember with Band II shifted to ~2.20um, further than standard HCP, reflecting maximum Ca content in the pyroxene structure.

distinguish_from.Phyllosilicate: Zeolite has similar 1.4+1.9um water bands but different 2.2-2.5um region; lacks Metal-OH absorption

Zeolite.mars_context: Possible detection in some altered terrains; indicates low-grade metamorphism
Zeolite.spectral_description: Hydrated aluminosilicate framework mineral. Water features at 1.4+1.9um similar to phyllosilicates, but lacks diagnostic Metal-OH bands in 2.2-2.35um region.

distinguish_from.Gypsum: Sulfate mix lacks gypsum-specific 1.75um and 2.2um triplet
distinguish_from.Kieserite: Sulfate mix at 1.93um vs kieserite shifted to 2.07-2.13um

Sulfate mix.mars_context: Valles Marineris; complex sulfate deposits with multiple phases
Sulfate mix.spectral_description: Generic polyhydrated sulfate when specific mineral (gypsum, epsomite, etc.) cannot be determined. Characterized by 1.93um H2O band at polyhydrated position.

distinguish_from.Saponite: Serpentine has weak/absent 1.9um H2O and unique 2.11um band; saponite has strong 1.9um

Serpentine.mars_context: Nili Fossae; formed by serpentinization of olivine-rich rocks with water
Serpentine.spectral_description: Mg-rich phyllosilicate with diagnostic 2.11um band unique among phyllosilicates. Weak or absent 1.9um H2O band distinguishes from hydrated smectites. Formed by hydration of olivine.

distinguish_from.LCP: Equivalent to LCP class; low-Ca pyroxene alternate designation

Pyroxene (low-Ca).spectral_description: Same as LCP class. Low-calcium pyroxene with Band II at ~1.83um.

band_assignments.1.02: Fe2+ crystal field (shifted shorter by higher Mg/Fe ratio)

distinguish_from.Olivine: Fo-rich has band center shifted to shorter wavelength (~1.02 vs ~1.05um) due to less Fe

Olivine (Fo-rich).spectral_description: Forsterite-rich olivine with band center shifted to shorter wavelength than Fe-rich olivine. Higher Mg/Fe ratio reduces Fe2+ absorption intensity and shifts band center blueward.

Water-unrelated.mars_context: Background material lacking diagnostic water-related mineral absorptions
Water-unrelated.spectral_description: No diagnostic absorption features related to water-bearing or hydroxyl-bearing minerals. May represent dust, unaltered basalt, or spectrally bland surfaces.
